title: Linux Administration Quiz
description: Test your Linux command-line, system administration, and troubleshooting skills.

questions.question: Which command shows disk usage of each directory summarized?
What does 'chmod 755 script.sh' set?
options: A. Owner: rwx, Group: r-x, Others: r-x
B. Owner: rwx, Group: rwx, Others: rwx
C. Owner: rw-, Group: r--, Others: r--
D. Owner: rwx, Group: r--, Others: ---

questions.question: In LVM, what is the correct order of creation?
Which command shows TCP listening ports with process names?
What does 'set -euo pipefail' do in a bash script?
options: A. Sets environment variables for production
B. Exit on error, treat unset vars as errors, fail on pipe errors
C. Sets file permissions for the script

questions.question: Which systemd command shows the last 50 lines of nginx service logs?
What does SUID bit do on an executable file?
options: A. Allows any user to delete the file
B. The file runs with the file owner's permissions, not the executing user's
C. Prevents the file from being executed by others
D. Sets the file as a system-critical binary

questions.question: What command displays SELinux security contexts on files?
How do you extend an ext4 logical volume online without unmounting?
What is RAID 5 minimum disk requirement and how many disk failures can it survive?
Which SSH option creates a local port forward (access remote service locally)?
What does the sticky bit on /tmp prevent?
options: A. Creating new files in the directory
B. Only allows the file owner or root to delete/rename their own files, even if others have write permission
C. Makes the directory read-only for everyone
D. Prevents all executables in the directory from running

questions.question: What does 'getenforce' return when SELinux is actively blocking violations?
Which command shows the process tree with PIDs?
What does 'kill -HUP PID' signal do to a running process?
options: A. Force kills the process immediately (SIGKILL)
C. Sends SIGHUP — typically causes the process to reload its configuration

questions.question: In /etc/passwd, what does 'x' in the password field mean?
options: C. The hashed password is stored in /etc/shadow

questions.question: What is the USE method for Linux performance analysis?
options: B. Utilization, Saturation, Errors — check each for every resource
C. User, System, Exit codes per process

questions.question: Which file defines filesystem mounts that persist across reboots?
What does 'chattr +i /etc/resolv.conf' do to the file?
options: A. Makes the file executable by all users
B. Makes the file immutable — even root cannot modify or delete it
C. Sets the file as hidden from ls output
D. Changes file to inherit parent directory's permissions

questions.question: What does 'nginx -t' do?
options: A. Tests connectivity to upstream servers
B. Starts nginx in test mode with minimal traffic
C. Validates nginx configuration file syntax without starting
D. Shows nginx active connections and throughput

questions.question: Which cron expression runs a job every day at 3:30 AM?
With default umask 022, what permissions do newly created files get?
How do you permanently allow port 8080 for Nginx in SELinux?
What does 'lvextend -l +100%FREE /dev/vg/lv' do?
options: A. Extends the logical volume by 100MB
B. Extends the LV to use all free space remaining in the volume group
C. Extends the volume group by 100% of current size
D. Creates a new logical volume using 100% of the disk

questions.question: Which systemd unit type replaces traditional cron jobs?
options: D. .timer unit paired with a .service unit
